| 052 | 2026-05-31 Phase-B BUILD subA adversary F-A2 (WARN) | **D-PHASE-B-DIFFCLASS-DESC-GRANULARITY.** `short_description` is classed as CONTENT alongside name/aliases, so a description-only edit makes the content hash differ ŌåÆ `diff_class="boundary"`. But `boundary` is meant for name/alias rename signal; glossary PATCH is *mostly* description edits, so `boundary` over-counts in `/corrections/stats` (feeds future eval-gold/few-shot tiers). Matches the locked design ┬¦3 (single content hash), so deferred not blocking. Fix: split a name/alias hash from the description hash (schema add) so `boundary` fires only on name/alias deltas; route description-only edits to `other`. Analytics-only, no write-path impact. | Phase D/E (when stats feed tiers) | LOW |
| 051 | 2026-05-31 Phase-B BUILD subA adversary F-A1 (cross-ref) | **D-PLATFORM-CONSUMER-RECLAIM-KNOWLEDGE.** The dead-retry-in-steady-state bug F-A1 (handler failure leaves a message pending in the PEL; `XREADGROUP ">"` never redelivers it; retry counter never advances until restart) was FIXED in learning-service via a periodic `XAUTOCLAIM` reclaim. **knowledge-service's `consumer.py` has the identical cloned pattern** and the same latent bug ŌĆö port the reclaim there too (less critical: glossary_sync is re-convergeable, but chapter/chat extraction events can also stall). | knowledge-service hardening | MED |
| 050 | 2026-05-31 Phase-B design ┬¦3 (review-impl R2 / PO redact-now) | **D-PHASE-E-RAW-CONTENT-OPTIN.** Phase B redacts by default: `corrections` stores `*_structural` + `*_content_hash`, NOT raw novel text (`before_content`/`after_content` reserved, NULL). Tier-3 organic eval-gold (plan ┬¦2.2.3) needs the *raw* before/after text ŌåÆ a per-tenant raw-retention opt-in that populates the reserved content columns, strictly owner-isolated + retention-bounded. Until opt-in, a tenant's corrections feed Tiers 1/2 (anchor/few-shot via hash+structural) but cannot become raw gold. | Phase E (organic gold) | MED |
| 049 | 2026-05-31 Phase-B design ┬¦10.1 (review-impl MED-1 / PO build-replay-now) | **D-PHASE-B-REPLAY-TOOL.** worker-infra-hosted `ReplayCorrections` admin task: given a time window, re-XADDs correction-typed `event_log` rows (full payload retained, `UNIQUE(source_service, source_outbox_id)`) back to their streams; learning-service re-consumes idempotently (`ON CONFLICT`). Makes a learning-service-outage-induced loss recoverable. Depends on: high `streamMaxLen` for glossary/knowledge (Ōēł200k) + bumped `event_log`/`OUTBOX_CLEANUP_RETAIN_DAYS` (Ōēł30d) + a `learning-collector` consumer-lag alert. | Phase B BUILD sub-session A/B | MED |
| 048 | 2026-05-31 Phase-B design (plan ┬¦2.2.1 / ┬¦4) | **D-PHASE-C-TIER1-ANCHOR-CONSUME.** Tier-1 dynamic anchor update ŌĆö corrections flowing into the per-project anchor index so the NEXT extraction reuses corrected canonical names/kinds/aliases. Per plan ┬¦4 this is **Phase C** (`depends_on: B`); the session-74 handoff conflated it into Phase B. Glossary-entity corrections already partly self-heal (Pass-0 rebuilds the anchor index from live `glossary list_entities` + `glossary_sync` MERGE), so the genuine gap is KS-only (non-anchored) entity corrections + relation/event corrections feeding back. Build after the Phase-B capture spine ships + correction volume accrues. | Phase C | MED |
| 047 | 2026-05-31 Phase-B design (plan ┬¦2.1 / ┬¦2.4) | **D-PHASE-B2-CONFIG-TELEMETRY.** Axis-2 config-adjustment telemetry: `config_registry` (content-addressed) + `config_adjustment_events` (async/lossy-OK) + `extraction_runs.outcome` + default versioning, in the new learning-service DB. | 046 | 2026-05-31 Phase-B design ┬¦6.6 (cross-store) | **D-PHASE-B-CORRECTION-RECONCILE.** KS graph edits are Neo4j; the correction outbox row is Postgres ŌåÆ no true transactional atomicity. Emit is best-effort post-Neo4j-success (try/except, WARN on failure). A dropped row under-counts the corrections log (never corrupts the graph). Hardening: a reconciliation sweep comparing Neo4j `user_edited`/`updated_at` nodes vs `corrections` rows to backfill misses. Acceptable to defer for Phase B (Tier-3 gold already needs an agreement/confidence gate). **Note (review-impl LOW-5): a dropped MIDDLE correction is a chain-BREAK, not just under-count** ŌĆö correction N+1's `before` no longer matches N's `after`; any future history-reconstruction-by-chaining must tolerate gaps. | Phase E (when corrections feed eval-gold) | LOW |
| 045 | 2026-05-31 Phase-B design ┬¦11 | **D-PHASE-B-LIVE-SMOKE.** Cross-service live smoke for the capture spine (Ōēź2 services ŌåÆ live-smoke token required): stack-up ŌåÆ user PATCH glossary entity via gateway ŌåÆ relay ships enriched `glossary.entity_updated` (actor=user) ŌåÆ learning-service `corrections` row appears with before/after + diff_class; then KS entity archive ŌåÆ `knowledge.entity_corrected` ŌåÆ corrections row. Runs at BUILD VERIFY (per sub-session). Logged now so the BUILD agent treats it as a must-do, not a happy-path skip. | Phase B BUILD VERIFY | MED |
| 044 | 2026-05-30 lore-enrichment C5 (D4-03 wiki-from-KG) | **Wire `KNOWLEDGE_SERVICE_URL` for glossary-service in `infra/docker-compose.yml`.** C5 added an optional `KNOWLEDGE_SERVICE_URL` config to glossary-service; the wiki renderer reads an entity's KG neighborhood from knowledge-service when it is set, and degrades to a name/kind/attributes-only body when it is unset (backward-compatible by design ŌĆö no hard dependency). The standard compose `glossary-service` env block does NOT set this var (C5 isolation rule forbade touching infra). Until it is wired, `/wiki/generate` in the dev/prod stack produces bodies WITHOUT the KG relationship sections. Live-smoke confirmed the full path works once the env is set (proven via a temporary compose override). Fix: add `KNOWLEDGE_SERVICE_URL: http://knowledge-service:8092` to the `glossary-service` environment block. ~1 line. | At C5 merge-time (platform-edit merge strategy per OPEN_QUESTIONS_LOCKED) OR C18 productionize | MED |
| 042 | 2026-05-30 lore-enrichment C0 adversary r2 WARN#2 | **`/health` DB-readiness probe.** `lore-enrichment-service` `/health` is a constant-`ok` **liveness** probe (matches chat-service/knowledge-service convention). | 041 | 2026-05-29 chunk-D /review-impl LOW-2 (decoration-placer arc) + 2026-05-30 chunk-C /review-impl LOW-3 (biome-theme arc) | **AC-DECO-8 + AC-BIOME-8 CI smoke gate.** Both e2e tests currently skip-tolerant on backend absence (default CI cross-browser job doesn't start tilemap-service). | 018 | 2026-05-16 tilemap Phase 1 human-in-loop review HIGH-1 | `penrose::assign_zone_tiles` is O(tiles ├Ś vertices) ŌĆö `nearest_vertex` linearly scans the full Penrose vertex field for every grid tile. At Continent scale (256┬▓ = 65 536 tiles ├Ś ~500-1200 vertices) that is ~10ŌüĘ-10ŌüĖ `distance_sq` calls, single-threaded, vs the TMP_002 ┬¦7 budget. Structurally the same defect as #016 (fractalize) but larger ŌĆö the tile loop runs over the whole grid, not one zone ŌĆö and the capped AMAW Adversary caught only #016. Invisible at the 24┬▓-64┬▓ test fixtures. Fix: bucket vertices into a coarse uniform grid and test only neighbour buckets, or a multi-source BFS distance transform ŌĆö output stays identical. **2026-05-18 continent measurement (finding O-1) confirms the pain: a 256┬▓ `place_tilemap` takes ~8ŌĆō11 min release-build ŌĆö recommend promoting to active work.** | Perf ŌĆö **profiling now shows pain (2026-05-18)** | MED |

| 016 | 2026-05-16 tilemap Phase 1 Adversary r1 WARN-2 | `fractalize::scatter_and_connect` is O(candidates┬▓) ŌĆö `nearest_cleared_dist_sq` linearly scans the growing `cleared` mask for every candidate. | 024 | 2026-05-24 v2.1e ship (geo-generator-amaw branch) | W9 shipped as amplitude-gated RGB shading (zone relief auto-scales intensity 0ŌåÆ1 across PlainsŌåÆMountains; ┬▒15% RGB modulation). Hypothesis "naive RGB regressed because eval framework had a problem" CONFIRMED: under v4.3 ecotone-aware eval, naive RGB amplitude-gated produces only ŌłÆ0.24 mean (neutral, 0 hard regressions) vs the v2.1b attempt's hard regression. Visual benefit currently limited (visible only on Ice zones where white amplifies modulation) but ships as foundation for future 3D map rendering. HSL approach not needed. True slope-aware hillshade (spec option B) still deferred to v3+. |
| 027 | 2026-05-22 tilemap test hardening | Added `a_bridge_mid_river_resets_the_periodic_ford_counter` (river_placer.rs): a 30├Ś3 straight river with a road at x=6; asserts the bridge resets the shared `since_crossing` counter, so the periodic ford lands at x=18 (12 carves after the bridge) ŌĆö NOT x=12 (which would mean the bridge didn't reset). Closes the interleaving coverage gap between `a_long_river_gets_periodic_fords` (pure every-Nth) and `a_river_crossing_a_road...` (pure bridge). Test-only; no output change. |
| 026 | 2026-05-22 tilemap river barrier reorder | Root cause refined: **erosion** (not fill) fragments the passable region, and the river's Mountain/Lake sources/sinks required erosion first ŌĆö so the river was structurally stuck threading post-erosion narrow channels (forded most tiles). Fix: split `ObstaclePlacer` into `ObstacleSourcePlacer` (places Mountain/Lake markers on Open tiles **pre-erosion**, dual-gated per-zone + map-wide) ŌåÆ `RiverPlacer` (carves the wide-open zone) ŌåÆ `ObstacleFillPlacer` (erode + fill the rest post-river, skipping river Water; erosion also skips Water fords). Golden fixture river ford ratio: **~0.77 crossings ŌåÆ 0.06 fords** (AC-2 gates < 0.25). Cost tradeoff accepted: continent 6.46 s ŌåÆ 10.56 s (river now carves a real barrier). Strict dual `would_seal_a_gap` gate unchanged. `/review-impl` caught a HIGH (source placer needed the map-wide gate arm, not per-zone only) ŌĆö fixed + regression-tested. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-22-tilemap-river-barrier-reorder.md`](../specs/2026-05-22-tilemap-river-barrier-reorder.md). |
| 029 | 2026-05-21 tilemap perf fix | `place_and_connect_object` refactored to **score-first, validate-on-demand**: cheap-filter + score all candidates (no flood fill), sort by `(score desc, flat asc)`, walk in best-first order and validate (`would_seal_a_gap` + `find_access_path`) lazily. The first sorted candidate that passes IS the winner (spec ┬¦4 proof: `first Ōłł V` of sorted `S` == `argmax_{v Ōłł V} (score, -flat)`). Naive kept under `#[cfg(test)]` as oracle. **Measurement: `treasure_placer` 973.376 s ŌåÆ 2.944 s (330├Ś); continent total 992.963 s ŌåÆ 21.541 s (46├Ś).** Golden test passes byte-exact ŌĆö no rebaseline. AC-1 oracle: 405 agreements across 3 fixtures ├Ś 3 templates ├Ś 3 optimisations ├Ś 3 min-distances ├Ś 5 sequential placements. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-21-tilemap-place-and-connect-perf.md`](../specs/2026-05-21-tilemap-place-and-connect-perf.md). |
| 018 | 2026-05-20 tilemap perf fix | `penrose::nearest_vertex` replaced with a bucket-based spiral search (`build_vertex_buckets` + `nearest_vertex_bucketed` over the new `UniformBuckets<Vec2>` helper in `engine/placement/spatial.rs`). Bit-exact equivalent to the prior linear scan (AC-2 oracle: 800 random queries ├Ś 4 vertex-field configurations incl. 1500-vertex dense; AC-3 cross-bucket tie-break by lowest index). Per-stage continent measurement: `place_zones` (Penrose + fractalize) dropped to **0.110 s** at 256┬▓ ŌĆö well under the TMP_002 ┬¦7 <500 ms budget. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md`](../specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md). |
| 016 | 2026-05-20 tilemap perf fix | `fractalize::scatter_and_connect` replaced with a bucket-based "any within radius" rewrite using the same `UniformBuckets<TileCoord>` helper ŌĆö 3├Ś3 ring neighbourhood query (sufficiency proven: `bucket_size = ceil(sqrt(coverage_sq))` ŌćÆ ring-2 min dist┬▓ Ōēź 49 > 36 surface / 25 > 16 sea). Bit-exact equivalent to the prior `nearest_cleared_dist_sq` linear scan (AC-1 oracle: 96┬▓ zone ├Ś 5 seeds ├Ś 2 spans). The naive impl is kept under `#[cfg(test)]` as the oracle. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md`](../specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md). |
| 022 | 2026-05-18 tilemap Phase E | Resolved by the Phase-E `RiverPlacer` design (spec D-Q5/D-Q6): the `anchor` representative point **suffices** ŌĆö no footprint extent needed. River sources are one lowest-flat-index `Mountain` obstacle anchor per mountain-bearing zone; sinks are the `Lake` obstacle anchors Ōł¬ `Sea`-zone tiles. `search_path` routes to/from the anchor tile, which is added to the river search area. No additive `TilemapObjectPlacement` footprint field was required. |
| 019 | 2026-05-18 tilemap Phase D | Re-validated when ConnectionsPlacer landed. `seal_borders` (┬¦3.1/┬¦9) seals inter-zone borders but is not a complete wall ŌĆö passage corridors stay `Walkable` and gap-critical tiles stay `Open`. The per-zone `would_seal_a_gap` is sound regardless: every tile belongs to exactly one zone, and every passability-removing mutation (placer objects, monoliths, guards, ferries, border seals) targets an `Open` tile that is zone-interior ŌĆö so removing a zone-X tile can never split zone Y. Border-seal completeness is irrelevant to correctness; the original "border thickness" framing was the wrong lens. |
| 013 | 2026-05-17 GEO Phase 2 | Connectivity-aware `choose_sea_level` ŌĆö binary-searches `sea_level` so the largest land component Ōēł the profile target (`maxcomp(L(s))` is monotone non-increasing in `s`), plus a continental base dome for the high-land `Inland` profile (a blob-only heightmap cannot form a connected 70% landmass). Realised land fraction is now within ┬▒0.08 of target, or a documented 8192 best-effort floor (┬▒0.18). Verified by `land_fraction_near_target` (16-seed sweep ├Ś 4 profiles). |
| 012 | 2026-05-16 tilemap fix task | Added `TestStreamOpenAICompat_MultipleToolCalls` ŌĆö two tool calls streamed with interleaved/non-monotonic `tool_calls[].index` (0,1,1,0); asserts `Index` is verbatim from the provider and per-index reassembly stays separate. |
| 011 | 2026-05-16 tilemap fix task | Dropped `nullable: true` from openapi `ChatStreamRequest.tool_choice` ŌĆö the field is now `description` + `oneOf` only (idiomatic OpenAPI 3.0; still optional via not-in-`required`). No runtime change; SDKs already model it optional. |
| 005 | 2026-05-15 tilemap Phase 0b /amaw | First full-feature `/amaw` dogfood (XL, 12 phases). 6 sub-agent spawns (4 design Adversary + 1 code Adversary + 1 Scope Guard) all completed their Step-0 `python scripts/mcp-query.py` calls cleanly ŌĆö no permission-prompt interruption. The allow-list is sufficient. |
| 003 | 2026-05-15 amaw-atomic-save-state | First `/amaw` dogfood. `save_state` now writes a PID-unique `.tmp` then `Path.replace()` (atomic rename); `cmd_reset` sweeps stale tmps; `finally`-unlink cleans own tmp. Adversary r1 added 3 hardening WARNs (all fixed). |
| 006 | 2026-05-15 batch pre-flight | AMAW.md Adversary + Scope Guard prompt templates now pre-instruct the Bash-heredoc fallback for writing findings/post-review docs, since the Write tool blocks sub-agents from writing report files. |
| 043 | 2026-05-30 C4 independent-adversary WARN-1 | **Manual single-entity name-fill does not propagate to Neo4j.** glossary `createEntity` emits an empty-name event (consumer skips it); the `PATCH /entities/{id}/attributes/{attr_value_id}` path (`attribute_handler.go patchAttributeValue`) that fills the name never calls `emitEntityUpdated`, so a manually-authored entity reaches Neo4j only when an unrelated `patchEntity` (status/tags/pin) fires. The enrichment DEMO path (extract-entities bulk, `extraction_handler.go`) emits the name inline and IS live-smoke-confirmed ŌĆö unaffected. Fix: add `s.emitEntityUpdated(ctx, entityID, "updated")` at the end of the setClauses block in `services/glossary-service/internal/api/attribute_handler.go` (~:159), mirroring `patchEntity`. | Platform completeness ŌĆö when manual single-entity authoring needs KG propagation | MED |
